page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The CPU includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, and FMA. This instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the ecs.se1.xlarge delivers modest performance relative to modern hardware. In compute benchmarks, the instance scored 1100.49 on stress-ng single-core and 2931.93 on multi-core tests, placing it in the bottom 25% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth benchmarks also reflect lower-tier performance, with cached small-block reads reaching 163.82 GB/sec, while uncached large-block reads and writes drop to 20.19 GB/sec and 18.14 GB/sec, respectively. Similarly, database operations and static web serving throughput are positioned in the bottom 10%, with web serving throughput measured at 2.41 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.se1.xlarge provides a dedicated CPU allocation and a generous memory footprint without the overhead of a high vCPU count. However, the trade-off is its lower compute and memory throughput. This makes the instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high CPU performance, such as lightweight caching layers, small database hosting, or memory-heavy background tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz and 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core but lacks local storage. Benchmark data places its compute and memory bandwidth performance in the lower tiers, with stress-ng and PassMark scores falling in the bottom 25%, and large-block memory reads in the bottom 10%. Consequently, the ecs.se1.xlarge is best suited for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads such as lightweight caching, small databases, and memory-heavy background processing.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
